summary: Problem 6 (Spielman) asks whether a universal constant c exists certifying ε-light subsets in graphs, an open research-level question with an estimated 18% solvability and a conjectured-tight target of c=1/2. The current best proof attempt (claude_opus_5-4) reports 100 open issues despite all 16 tracked granular issues being marked resolved, indicating a substantial divergence between the issue tracker and the proof's actual completeness. The resolved issues cluster around the technical machinery of a barrier-method/Tr_sigma argument (Ky Fan principles, barrier update lemma, leverage-score invariants), but no single coherent, gap-free proof has been assembled.
problems: Status mismatch: 'Best Proof' reports 100 open issues while all 16 tracked issues are marked resolved — the proof is not actually complete and the tracker is not authoritative.

Core mathematical blocker is the existence of a universal constant c independent of G; the barrier/Tr_sigma framework yields per-instance bounds but has not been shown to produce a graph-independent constant.

The barrier-method spine (Barrier Update Lemma, Tr_sigma summation, Pi_S decomposition) required many sequential fixes — rank assumptions, symmetrization conventions, negative pseudo-powers, Ky Fan max vs subadditivity — signaling the argument is fragile and interdependent.

Distinct conventions for Tr_sigma(M^p B) were used before being defined and across multiple steps; even after fixes, consistency of the symmetrized convention across the whole proof is unverified end-to-end.

The Greedy Lemma's connection to the Barrier Update Lemma was only recently made explicit; the global induction that chains local barrier updates into a final c=1/2-type bound is the least-verified part.
The conjectured tightness c=1/2 is unproven — no matching lower-bound construction is documented.
highlights: All 16 granular technical issues were individually addressed by solver-agent with detailed, targeted fixes (rank arguments, foundational reduction ker L ⊆ ker L_S, Foster's identity for disconnected G).
Foundational reduction and Foster's identity were generalized beyond the connected case, removing an unwarranted connectivity assumption.
The Barrier Update Lemma was rewritten into 8 explicit verifiable steps, improving auditability of the most central lemma.
Ky Fan maximum principle and subadditivity are now cited and proved separately, eliminating a recurring conflation error.
The base case for the leverage-score invariant (critical issue) was fixed to handle an arbitrary initial vertex rather than assuming a convenient one.

suggested_todos.title: Reconcile the 100-open-issue 'Best Proof' status with the 16 resolved tracked issues; produce an authoritative list of remaining gaps
Perform an end-to-end consistency audit of the Tr_sigma symmetrized convention across all steps where M^p B appears
Verify the global induction chaining Greedy Lemma + Barrier Update Lemma actually yields a graph-independent constant c
Attempt a matching lower-bound construction to test the conjectured tightness c=1/2
Write a standalone, linear proof draft that assembles the resolved lemmas into one coherent argument without forward references
Document explicitly where (if anywhere) the per-instance bounds fail to be universal, to scope the remaining research blocker
Cross-check Step 4 decomposition after removal of the undefined negative pseudo-power to confirm no downstream step relies on the old form
